paragraphs.text: A quick chemistry note: the data show **strong Zn–histidine binding**, with \( \log K_1 \) around **6.3–7.1** and \( \log \beta_2 \) around **11.7–12.7**, consistent with histidine being an excellent Zn(II) ligand through amino/carboxylate/imidazole donor sets. The protonated complexes \( \mathrm{ZnHL} \) and \( \mathrm{ZnHL_2} \) indicate that speciation will be quite pH-sensitive near physiological conditions.

claims.text: histidine being an excellent Zn(II) ligand
speciation will be quite pH-sensitive near physiological conditions.
